textPlain: Very impressive enhancement. Not a panacea though. It uses tree-sitter approach to solve situations when two users change the same line of code. For example one change function name and other adds a new argument. It will merge it without conflicts. It still has some troubles to solve complex issues, without knowing author intensions. But can significantly simplify developers' lives. Not sure if it would land into git very soon. It requires all git to know all the parsers you need. But definitely worth adding.

This is a very interesting idea that could save a lot of time and pain in big projects.The example shown reminds me pf Zed's CRDTs [1], and their journey to build a fine-grained version control system  for agentic development [2]—I imagine this work could prove useful to the Zed/Cursor team, and likely shares a lot of functionality with DeltaDB [2].- [1]: https://zed.dev/blog/crdts- [2]: https://zed.dev/blog/sequoia-backs-zed
This is a seprate tool that one can tell git to use.
